subject: DevOps
title: Caching for Speed
description: A micro-lesson that makes Caching for Speed usable.

hook.text: The pipeline downloads 800 MB of dependencies every single run. That's five minutes of pure waste.

buildup.text: Dependency files rarely change between commits. Downloading them fresh each time is pointless.

discovery.text: Pipeline caching stores dependencies between runs. If the lockfile hasn't changed, skip the download.

twist.text: Cache keys are hash-based. Change the lockfile, and the cache invalidates automatically. Fresh deps when needed.

climax.text: Docker layer caching is even more powerful. Unchanged layers are reused, only modified layers rebuild.

punchline.text: Cache dependencies and layers. Spend pipeline time on real work.

quiz.question: When does a pipeline cache typically invalidate?
options: When the lockfile or cache key changes
